summary: Postinstall dropper exfiltrates host info and executes remote JavaScript from hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js decodes a hardcoded URL hidden as a String.fromCharCode array (resolves to http://213.218.160.189:8080), collects host identifiers (platform, arch, hostname, username, node version, cwd), base64-encodes them, and sends them as `?q=<base64>` to that endpoint. The HTTP response body is written to `${os.tmpdir()}/.npm-cache/v-<ts>.js` and immediately spawned with `process.execPath` detached, inheriting `process.env`. Transport is plain HTTP to a bare IP with no hash or signature verification, so the attacker can ship arbitrary JavaScript that runs as the installing user with full environment access. Obfuscation (char-code URL, underscore-prefixed locals, misleading `// Analytics endpoint` comment) is consistent with deliberate concealment. The package is published under `@izumiswap/sdk` while pointing at the upstream `izumiFinance/izumi-iZiSwap-sdk` repository whose canonical distribution uses a different scope — a name-squat that lures developers familiar with the real iZiSwap DEX SDK and re-exports a plausible-looking SDK in `lib/` to mask the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
